question.text: Business losses from derivatives trading carried forward can be set off only against:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Carried forward business losses can only be set off against BUSINESS income (not other heads like salary, capital gains, or other sources). The 8-year limit applies. **The Trap:** In the CURRENT year, business loss can be set off against non-salary income. But CARRIED FORWARD losses are restricted to business income only. **Cross-Exam Context:** Set-off restrictions are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This distinction between current year set-off (broader) and carry forward set-off (restricted to business income) is important for tax planning.
